content.problemStatement:
You are given the sizes of angles of a simple quadrilateral (in degrees) AAA, BBB, CCC and DDD, in some order along its perimeter. Determine whether the quadrilateral is cyclic.

Note: A quadrilateral is cyclic if and only if the sum of opposite angles is 180∘180^{\circ}180∘.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains four space‑separated integers A, B, C, D – the interior angles of a quadrilateral in order along its perimeter.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if the quadrilateral can be cyclic, otherwise "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: A+C = 10+30 = 40 ≠ 180 → NO.\nTest case 2: A+C = 10+170 = 180 → YES (automatically B+D = 20+160 = 180).\nTest case 3: A+C = 179+179 = 358 ≠ 180 → NO.

solution.approach: For a simple quadrilateral the sum of all interior angles is 360°. It is cyclic iff the sum of one pair of opposite angles equals 180°. Because the angles are given in order, the opposite pairs are (A, C) and (B, D). If A + C == 180, then automatically B + D == 180 (since total is 360). So we only need to check A + C == 180 for each test case and output YES or NO accordingly.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out = []
    for _ in range(T):
        A = int(next(it)); B = int(next(it)); C = int(next(it)); D = int(next(it))
        out.append('YES' if A + C == 180 else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
